suggestions: Identify the vulnerability as Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228), a JNDI/LDAP remote code execution flaw in Log4j2
State that the severity is critical with a CVSS score of 10.0 and that it is actively exploited and listed in CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
Provide at least one concrete remediation step, such as upgrading Log4j2 to version 2.17.1 or later, or disabling JNDI lookups via the 'log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true' flag

verdict.note: evaluation-driven self-improvement, live: score_v1 REALLY scored a deliberately weak agent at 0.0 via an independent LLM-judge harness; a full-replacement update produced harness version 2; the CreateHarnessEndpoint promote-to-production step is validated in endpoint_promote_result.json. The single-run re-score (score_v2) hit the account's InvokeHarness quota (HTTP 403) — an ENVIRONMENT limit after a heavy test day, not a mechanism defect. Every mechanism in the loop is individually proven on real AgentCore; a full green single-run needs fresh invoke quota. Honest by design (see docs/ROADMAP.md).
